metadata.notes: Content overhaul — Wingspan teaching standard. Sources: official rulebook, Stonemaier FAQ, Dized interactive rules, BGG forums, community strategy guides.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Boards & Mats
- **1 Basecamp board** — the central display with card slots and the glory track
- **20 hexagonal location tiles** in 3 regions: 6 South, 7 Central, 7 North
- **1 mech mat per player** — tracks your power, guile, workers, and action token position

#### Cards
- **123 uniquely illustrated cards** in a single shuffled deck, comprising 3 types:
  - **Item cards** — have an instant benefit and an ongoing ability; can be upgraded by tucking under your mat
  - **Meteorite cards** — have a meld ability; tuck under your mech mat for cascading bonuses
  - **Quest cards** — have a solve ability; require your mech to be at the matching location
- **1 character card + 1 companion card per player** (your starting hand)

#### Miniatures & Tokens
- **1 large mech miniature per player** (snaps onto a silicone base)
- **4 glory tokens (stars) per player** — you place these via Boast to trigger the endgame
- **1 action token (cube) per player** — sits on your mech mat to determine which actions you perform
- **Power and guile tracking tokens** per player
- **50 worker meeples** in 5 colors
- **36 corruption tiles + 1 cloth bag** — these get placed on locations during exploration and are worth $2 each when you vanquish them
- Map tokens, coins (limited supply)

#### Other Components
- 14 Automa cards (solo mode)
- 2 player aid cards per player
- Custom insert with setup tray

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Core Loop
subtopics.content:
#### Overview
Expeditions is a standalone sequel to Scythe, set in an alternate 1920s. A meteorite has crashed near the Tunguska River, scattering corruption across the wilderness. You pilot a mech into the unknown to explore locations, play cards to build your engine, gather resources, and vanquish corruption — which is a good thing, because corruption tokens are worth money at game end.

#### The 4 Actions
Your mech mat has 4 action spaces: **Move**, **Play**, **Gather**, and **Refresh**. The action token covers one space at a time; you perform the uncovered actions.

**After a Refresh**: Your action token sits on Refresh. Shift it left into the Move/Play/Gather area — all 3 actions are uncovered. Perform all 3 in any order.

**Subsequent turns**: Move the action token to cover exactly 1 of the 3 action spaces (Move, Play, or Gather). Perform the other 2 in any order. Each turn, you must shift the token to cover a different action than last turn.

**Refresh turn**: Instead of normal actions, return all your active row cards to your hand and all workers to your mat. Place the action token on Refresh. Your next turn, you'll get all 3 actions again.

#### The Card Engine
Cards flow between 3 zones:
- **Hand** (left of mech mat) — cards you can play
- **Active row** (right of mech mat) — cards currently in play, generating abilities
- **Tucked** (under mech mat edges) — upgraded items, melded meteorites, solved quests — these score permanently

#### Corruption: A Resource, Not a Punishment
Corruption tokens appear on locations when you explore them. You **vanquish** corruption by spending power and guile, then you **collect** the token — it's worth **$2 at game end**. Having **7+ corruption tokens** is one of the glory categories you can Boast for. Corruption is something you want to pursue, not avoid.

#### Game End
When any player places their **4th glory token** via Boast, the endgame triggers. Every player (including the one who triggered it) takes exactly 1 final turn. Most money wins.

subtopics.title: Detailed Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Setup
1. Place the Basecamp board centrally. Arrange the 6 South location tiles face-up per the layout diagram.
2. Shuffle Central (7) and North (7) tiles face-down in their spots. Place 1 map token on each face-down tile.
3. Shuffle all item, meteorite, and quest cards into one deck. Deal 5 cards face-up onto the Basecamp display.
4. Fill the corruption bag with all tokens except the 20-value token (set aside for Location 20).
5. Each player takes: mech mat + mech miniature, character card + companion card (into hand), 4 glory tokens, 1 action token (placed on Refresh space), power/guile tokens at zero.
6. Place all mechs adjacent to Basecamp. Randomly select first player.

#### The Four Actions

**MOVE** — Move your mech 1-3 spaces (Marsh Strider: 1-4). You can move through occupied spaces but cannot end on one. You cannot return to Basecamp after your first move.
- **Revealing tiles**: When you move onto a face-down tile, your movement ends immediately. Gain the map token on it. Flip the tile face-up. Draw corruption tokens from the bag until their total value meets or exceeds the location's printed value — stack them on the location. These corruption tokens are now waiting to be vanquished.

**PLAY** — Play exactly 1 card from your hand to the rightmost position in your active row. Then, in any order:
- Gain the **core value** (number in upper-left) — advance your power or guile (max 10).
- **Activate the ability** — place a matching-color worker from your mat onto the card. Items have instant + ongoing benefits. Quests have a Solve ability. Meteorites have a Meld ability.
- Worker color must match the card's requirement. Max 1 worker per card. Each card's ability activates at most once per turn.

**GATHER** — Gain all exposed benefits at your mech's current location. Corruption tokens cover benefits until you vanquish them. You must gain all exposed benefits if possible.
- Benefits include: gain a face-up card, sweep the display, gain a worker, gain a map token, play a card, upgrade an item, meld a meteorite, boast, and others.

**REFRESH** — Return all active row cards to your hand and all workers to your mat. Place your action token on Refresh. Next turn you'll get all 3 actions again.

#### Vanquish (Via Card Abilities, Not a Standalone Action)
Corruption tokens sit on locations, blocking the benefits underneath. To vanquish them:
- Pay the cost on the topmost corruption token at your mech's location using power and/or guile.
- **Take the token** — it's yours now, worth **$2 at game end**.
- You may repeat to vanquish additional tokens at the same location.
- When you remove the final corruption from a location, the hidden benefit underneath becomes available.
- **Location 20**: Requires BOTH 10 power AND 10 guile (not either/or). Still only worth $2.

Collecting corruption is **positive** — each token is $2, and having **7+ corruption tokens** qualifies you for one of the glory categories.

#### Solve (Quests)
- Your mech must be at the location matching the quest card.
- Pay the power/guile cost shown. Gain the benefit.
- Tuck the quest under the top edge of your mech mat. Max 4 solved quests.

#### Upgrade (Items)
- Tuck an item from your hand or active row under the right edge of your mech mat.
- The ongoing ability becomes permanently active (no worker needed).
- Max 4 upgraded items. The coin value on the item scores at game end.

#### Meld (Meteorites)
- Tuck a meteorite under the bottom edge of your mech mat.
- Immediately gain the meld bonus of ALL currently melded meteorites (cascading). Each successive meld grows more powerful.
- Max 4 melded meteorites.

#### Boast (Placing Glory Stars)
When you've achieved a glory category, you can place a glory star on it:
- **7 categories:** Quests (4 solved), Meteorites (4 melded), Items (4 upgraded), Location 20 (vanquished), Corruption (7+ tokens collected), Cards (8+ controlled), Workers/Maps (7+ workers OR 5+ map tokens).
- Multiple players can have stars on the same category.
- You need only **4 of 7** categories to trigger the endgame.

#### Mech Abilities
- **Tatanka**: 5 slots for quests/melds/upgrades (instead of 4)
- **Marsh Strider**: Movement range 1-4 (instead of 1-3)
- **Lumberjack**: Reduce first corruption cost per color by 1
- **Highlander**: Gain cards to hand instead of active row
- **Odin's Wrath**: Trash a map token for 1 power, 1 guile, or $1

subtopics.title: Scoring & End Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game End Trigger
When any player places their **4th glory token** via Boast, the endgame triggers. Every player (including the triggering player) takes exactly **1 final turn**.

#### Final Scoring (All Money)
All scoring converts to money. Most money wins.

| Source | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Coins | Face value |
| Upgraded items | Coin value printed on card |
| Corruption tokens | $2 each (including the 20-value token) |
| Glory stars | Variable — scales with quests solved |

**Glory Star Value by Quests Solved:**
- 0 quests: $5 per star
- 1 quest: $6 per star
- 2 quests: $8 per star
- 3+ quests: **$10 per star**

With 4 stars and 3 solved quests, stars alone = $40. With 0 quests, stars = only $20. Solving quests is the single biggest scoring multiplier in the game.

#### Tiebreaker
1. Sum of all glory category values (totals across every category, including those without stars).
2. If still tied: shared victory.

#### Typical Score Ranges
- First game: $35-50
- Experienced play: $55-75
- Highly optimized: $80+

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Minimize Refresh Turns
Refreshing is essentially a lost turn — you produce nothing while everyone else advances. The best players time their Refreshes to squeeze maximum value from each Move/Play/Gather cycle. Every unnecessary Refresh is a turn your opponents are gaining on you.

#### Solve 3 Quests as Your Top Priority
This is the single biggest scoring multiplier in the game. Solving 3+ quests raises each glory star from $5 to $10 — meaning 4 stars go from $20 total to $40, a **$20 swing**. Even solving 2 quests ($8/star) is a significant improvement. Never ignore quests.

#### Plan Multiple Turns Ahead
Solving a quest requires 4 conditions to align simultaneously: (a) you control a quest card, (b) your mech is at the matching location, (c) you have enough power/guile, and (d) you have a card with a Solve ability to play. That takes careful multi-turn setup — start positioning early.

#### Meld Meteorites for Cascading Value
Each time you meld a meteorite, you gain the meld bonus of ALL previously melded meteorites plus the new one. The 4th meld triggers all 4 bonuses at once. Melding gets progressively more powerful — don't wait until late game to start.

#### Vanquish Corruption Aggressively
Corruption tokens are worth **$2 each** at game end, and collecting **7+ tokens** qualifies you for a glory category. That's potentially $14+ in tokens plus a glory star worth $5-10. Invest in power and guile early so you can vanquish efficiently as you explore.

#### Leverage Your Mech's Ability
- **Tatanka**: Go wide — 5 slots for quests/melds/upgrades gives you more flexibility than anyone else.
- **Marsh Strider**: Extra movement range (1-4) for positional advantage. Reach distant locations before opponents.
- **Highlander**: Cards go to hand instead of active row — gives you flexibility on timing plays.
- **Lumberjack**: Reduced corruption costs. Lean into vanquishing for the corruption glory category.
- **Odin's Wrath**: Convert excess map tokens into power, guile, or money.

#### Choose Your 4 Glory Paths Adaptively
You need only 4 of the 7 categories. Read the board state, your card draws, and opponents' progress. Pick the 4 categories that are most efficient for your current engine — don't chase all 7.

#### Watch the Endgame Trigger
The game ends when anyone places their 4th glory star. Be aware of opponents approaching that threshold. If someone is at 3 stars, you may need to accelerate your own Boasts or make sure your final turn scoring is maximized.

#### Common Mistakes
- **Refreshing too often.** Every Refresh is a lost turn. Build larger active rows before resetting.
- **Ignoring quests.** Zero solved quests means your 4 stars are worth only $20 instead of $40. Quests are not optional.
- **Avoiding corruption.** Corruption tokens are $2 each and count toward a glory category. Vanquish them — don't leave free money on the board.
- **Spreading too thin.** Focus on 4 achievable glory categories rather than half-completing 6.
- **Forgetting worker color matching.** Workers must match the card's color requirement to activate an ability.
- **Not gathering all exposed benefits.** You MUST gain all exposed benefits when Gathering. Plan your movement to land on locations where all exposed benefits are useful.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Expeditions

**Setup**: You start with your mech adjacent to Basecamp, a character card and companion card in hand, your action token on Refresh, and power/guile both at zero. The 6 South locations are face-up; Central and North tiles are face-down with map tokens on them.

**Turn 1 (All 3 Actions)**: Your action token is on Refresh, so shift it left — you get Move, Play, and Gather this turn. Move your mech to a nearby South location. Play your character card to your active row — gain its core value (advance power or guile) and activate its ability by placing a matching-color worker on it. Then Gather at your location to pick up an exposed benefit (maybe a face-up card or a worker).

**Turn 2 (2 Actions)**: Shift your action token to cover one of the three action spaces. If you cover Move, you stay put and Play + Gather. If you cover Gather, you Move + Play elsewhere. Choose based on what your current location offers and what's in your hand.

**Turns 3-5 — Explore and Build**: Play item and meteorite cards to grow your active row. Move to face-down tiles to explore — flip them face-up, gain map tokens, and watch corruption tokens stack on the revealed locations. Start building power and guile so you can vanquish that corruption for $2 per token.

**Mid-Game — Engine Running**: Your active row should have 3-4 cards generating abilities. When your workers are all placed and your hand is running low, take a Refresh turn — return everything to hand and mat, then get all 3 actions next turn.

**Quest Solving**: When you have a quest card, position your mech at the matching location, build enough power/guile to pay the cost, and play a card with a Solve ability. The quest tucks under your mat permanently. Aim for at least 3 solved quests — the scoring multiplier jumps from $5/star to $10/star.

**Vanquishing Corruption**: When your mech is at a location with corruption tokens, use card abilities to vanquish them — pay the power/guile cost shown on the token, then take it. Each token is worth $2. Clear all corruption from a location to unlock the hidden benefit underneath.

**Endgame Push**: Once you've achieved 4 glory categories (e.g., 4 solved quests, 4 upgraded items, 7+ corruption tokens, 8+ cards controlled), find Boast benefits during Gather actions and place your 4 stars. The game ends after everyone takes 1 final turn.

**Scoring**: Add up coins + upgraded item values + $2 per corruption token + glory star values (scaled by quests solved). First game target: $40-55 is solid.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Action Token Optimization
The action token system is the core strategic puzzle. You get all 3 actions after a Refresh, then 2 actions per turn until your next Refresh. Every Refresh is a lost turn of productivity.

- **Ideal cycle length: 4-5 turns per Refresh.** Build your active row to 4-5 cards before resetting. Shorter cycles waste Refresh turns; longer cycles mean your hand is empty and workers are stuck.
- **Cover Move when your location has multiple benefits.** Staying put to Play + Gather maximizes a rich location.
- **Cover Gather when you need to reposition.** Move + Play lets you travel and build your engine simultaneously.
- **Cover Play when a location has Boast or Vanquish benefits.** Move + Gather lets you reach a location and immediately collect.

#### Quest-First Engine Building
Solving 3+ quests makes every glory star worth $10 instead of $5. That's a $20 total swing across 4 stars. Structure your entire early game around quest completion:

1. **Acquire quest cards early** from the Basecamp display or via Gather benefits.
2. **Plot movement toward quest locations** while exploring nearby tiles.
3. **Build power/guile to meet quest costs** via your character, companion, and item cards.
4. **Time your Play action** to have a Solve ability available when your mech reaches the quest location.

#### Corruption as a Scoring Engine
Corruption tokens are worth **$2 each**, and collecting **7+ tokens** earns a glory star. A player who vanquishes 8 corruption tokens scores $16 in tokens plus a star worth $5-10. That makes corruption one of the most efficient glory paths.

- **Lumberjack** has a natural advantage — reduced corruption costs mean faster, cheaper vanquishing.
- **Invest in power AND guile.** Most corruption tokens accept either, but Location 20 requires both at max (10 power + 10 guile).
- **Explore aggressively** to spawn corruption on new tiles, then vanquish before opponents reach the same location.

#### Meld Cascading
Meteorities cascade: the 1st meld gives 1 bonus, the 2nd gives 2 bonuses, the 3rd gives 3, and the 4th gives all 4 bonuses at once. Total: 10 meld bonus triggers across 4 melds.

- **Front-load meteorite collection** so early melds start compounding.
- **Pair meteorites with complementary bonuses** — a meld that grants power + a meld that grants guile accelerates vanquishing.
- **Tatanka's 5th slot** for melds is especially strong — 5 cascading melds = 15 total bonus triggers.

#### Glory Path Selection
You need 4 of 7 categories. Evaluate which 4 are most efficient for your current board state:

- **Quests (4 solved):** Always pursue — the scoring multiplier affects ALL stars. The single most impactful category.
- **Corruption (7+ tokens):** Strong with Lumberjack or aggressive exploration. Worth pursuing if you're already vanquishing.
- **Items (4 upgraded):** Natural byproduct of engine building. Easy if you're drawing lots of items.
- **Meteorites (4 melded):** Cascading bonuses make this self-reinforcing. Strong with Tatanka.
- **Cards (8+ controlled):** Count hand + active row + tucked. Easy to reach passively by mid-game.
- **Workers/Maps (7+ workers OR 5+ maps):** Map tokens come from exploring face-down tiles. Workers from Gather benefits.
- **Location 20:** Requires maxing both power and guile. High cost but only 1 vanquish needed.

#### Endgame Trigger Timing
The game ends when someone places their 4th star. Everyone (including the trigger player) gets 1 final turn.

- **Trigger when you're ahead in money.** If your engine is generating more per turn than opponents', ending early locks in your advantage.
- **Delay by not Boasting** if you need 1-2 more turns to complete quests or vanquish high-value corruption.
- **Watch opponents at 3 stars.** If someone is one Boast away from triggering the end, make sure your final turn is planned.

rulings: The action token stays in the Move/Play/Gather box once placed there. You must Refresh to return to all-3-actions mode.
'Gain 1 location benefit' means exactly 1 benefit icon, even if the location shows multiple icons.
A card's ability may only activate once per turn unless the card specifically states otherwise.
Adjacency is always relative to the mech's physical position on the map, not to cards in your active row.
'Gain the core value of all solved quests' includes the quest being solved in the same action.
You must gain ALL exposed benefits when Gathering if possible. You cannot selectively skip them.
Power and guile cap at 10. Effects that would push above 10 stop at the cap.
The 20-value corruption requires BOTH 10 power AND 10 guile (not either/or). It is still only worth $2 at game end.
Worker color must match the card's color requirement to activate an ability.
Gaining a Refresh benefit from a card is NOT the same as taking the Refresh action — your action token does NOT move to the Refresh space.
